Unebi|arz|جبل مونت ونيبى|ceb|Unebi Yama|en|Mount Unebi|ja|畝傍山|lld|Mont Unebi|ru|Унэби (гора)|uk|Унебі (гора)|WD=Q17606448|1=Mount Unebi}} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Unebiyamaguchi-jinja1.jpg {{commons category|Category:Unebiyamaguchi-jinja}} [[JA:畝火山口神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11578590}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine | 画像=[[file:Unebiyamaguchi-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Ōtani-chō 248-5 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|29|33|N|135|46|52|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}
{{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（great）
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=April 16|Q2519|WD=Q2519|1=April 16}} | 神事=Hani-tsuchi ritual | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Unebi-yama
Yamaguchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Unebiyamaguchi Shrine''' (うねびやまぐちじんじゃ, おむねやまじんじゃOn the signboard in front of the Mt. Unebi trailhead by the shrine, “畝火山口” is given the reading “おむねやま”.) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} on the western foot of {{ill|lt=Mount Unebi|Q17606448|WD=Q17606448|1=Mount Unebi}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is popularly called “Omine-yama.” It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was prefectural shrine. It is one of the six Yamaguchi shrines in Yamato listed in the norito of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} ({{ill|lt=Asuka Shrine#Precincts|Q11665959|WD=Q11665959|lt=Asuka|1=Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine}}, Ishimura, Osa, Hase, '''Unebi''', and {{ill|lt=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11610732|WD=Q11610732|lt=Miminashi|1=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine}}), and one of the 85 shrines of the rain-prayer rites in the {{ill|lt=special festivals|Q11612499|WD=Q11612499|1=special festivals}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}), {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}}, and {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi three deities|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}. Based on the tradition that Empress Jingū gave birth to Emperor Ōjin, it is worshiped as a deity of safe childbirth. From examples of other {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi shrines|Q11467399|WD=Q11467399|1=Yamaguchi shrines}} and the shrine’s character, the original deity is thought to have been {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}. Today Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in the auxiliary Ōyamatsumi Shrine. === History === The origin of the foundation is unknown. The first documentary appearance is in ''Shinshō Kakuchokufu-shō'', which records one shrine household granted in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|1=Daidō}} 1). According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', in {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}} ({{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|1=Jōgan}} 1), on {{ill|lt=January 27 (lunar)|Q839032|WD=Q839032|lt=1st month 27th day|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)}}, it received {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of Senior Fifth Lower, and on {{ill|lt=September 8 (lunar)|Q840354|WD=Q840354|lt=September 8|1=September 8 (Lunisolar)}} of the same year an {{ill|lt=imperial envoy with offerings|Q11445508|WD=Q11445508|1=Hōhei}} was dispatched for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=rain prayer|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it appears as “Unebi Yamaguchi-za Shrine, Takachi District, Yamato Province,” is listed as a great shrine, and is recorded as receiving offerings at monthly and Niiname rites. Originally it stood on the western foot of Mt. Unebi. In the ''Gogun Jinjaki'' of {{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1446|Q6430|WD=Q6430|1=1446}}), it is described as “at Kume-gō, western mountain tail of Mt. Unebi.” But maps of the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era place it on the summit, meaning it was moved from the foot to the summit between Bun'an and Tenshō. The shrine name evolved from Unebi-za Yamaguchi Shrine; in the Edo period it was also called “Unebi Myōjin” and “Unebi-yama Jingū-sha.” In {{ill|lt=1940|Q18633|WD=Q18633|1=1940}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 15), when major expansion works were carried out at {{ill|lt=Kashihara Jingū|Q710333|WD=Q710333|1=Kashihara Shrine}} on the eastern foot of Mt. Unebi, it was deemed, in the climate of the high tide of {{ill|lt=imperial-state historiography|Q5368837|WD=Q5368837|1=Kōkoku shikan}}, that looking down over Kashihara Jingū and Emperor Jinmu’s mausoleum would “defile divine authority”According to the explanatory notice at the torii of Unebiyamaguchi Shrine.. The Japanese government therefore ordered relocation to the western foot (current site). At that time it was promoted from {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} to prefectural shrine. === Rituals === A special ritual called “Hani-tsuchi ritual” is held. It takes soil from the summit of Mt. Unebi for making earthenware used at {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} in the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} (February) and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} (November). On that occasion, those performing the rite customarily prepare their ritual garments at {{ill|lt=Kawamata Shrine|Q11553360|WD=Q11553360|1=Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}} before heading to this shrine. === Events === * January 1: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=New Year’s Day festival|1=UNKNOWN}} * February 28: {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} - Otaue festival (onda matsuri) * April 16: Spring grand festival - Daidai festival * July 28: Summer grand festival - Densoso festival[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/saijiki/7_densosomatsuri.html Densoso festival (Kashihara City official site)] * July 29: Great purification festival * December 3: New grain thanksgiving festival * 1st of every month: {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}}, 15th: Birth report festival === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === External links === {{commons-inline|Category:Unebiyamaguchi-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うねひやまくちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unebiyamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unebiyamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Unebiyamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大うねひやまくちしんしや]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{commons category|Category:Unebiyamaguchi-jinja}} [[JA:畝火山口神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11578590}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine | 画像=[[file:Unebiyamaguchi-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Ōtani-chō 248-5 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|29|33|N|135|46|52|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}
{{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（great）
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=April 16|Q2519|WD=Q2519|1=April 16}} | 神事=Hani-tsuchi ritual | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Unebi-yama
Yamaguchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Unebiyamaguchi Shrine''' (うねびやまぐちじんじゃ, おむねやまじんじゃOn the signboard in front of the Mt. Unebi trailhead by the shrine, “畝火山口” is given the reading “おむねやま”.) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} on the western foot of {{ill|lt=Mount Unebi|Q17606448|WD=Q17606448|1=Mount Unebi}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is popularly called “Omine-yama.” It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was prefectural shrine. It is one of the six Yamaguchi shrines in Yamato listed in the norito of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} ({{ill|lt=Asuka Shrine#Precincts|Q11665959|WD=Q11665959|lt=Asuka|1=Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine}}, Ishimura, Osa, Hase, '''Unebi''', and {{ill|lt=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11610732|WD=Q11610732|lt=Miminashi|1=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine}}), and one of the 85 shrines of the rain-prayer rites in the {{ill|lt=special festivals|Q11612499|WD=Q11612499|1=special festivals}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}), {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}}, and {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi three deities|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}. Based on the tradition that Empress Jingū gave birth to Emperor Ōjin, it is worshiped as a deity of safe childbirth. From examples of other {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi shrines|Q11467399|WD=Q11467399|1=Yamaguchi shrines}} and the shrine’s character, the original deity is thought to have been {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}. Today Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in the auxiliary Ōyamatsumi Shrine. === History === The origin of the foundation is unknown. The first documentary appearance is in ''Shinshō Kakuchokufu-shō'', which records one shrine household granted in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|1=Daidō}} 1). According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', in {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}} ({{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|1=Jōgan}} 1), on {{ill|lt=January 27 (lunar)|Q839032|WD=Q839032|lt=1st month 27th day|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)}}, it received {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of Senior Fifth Lower, and on {{ill|lt=September 8 (lunar)|Q840354|WD=Q840354|lt=September 8|1=September 8 (Lunisolar)}} of the same year an {{ill|lt=imperial envoy with offerings|Q11445508|WD=Q11445508|1=Hōhei}} was dispatched for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=rain prayer|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it appears as “Unebi Yamaguchi-za Shrine, Takachi District, Yamato Province,” is listed as a great shrine, and is recorded as receiving offerings at monthly and Niiname rites. Originally it stood on the western foot of Mt. Unebi. In the ''Gogun Jinjaki'' of {{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1446|Q6430|WD=Q6430|1=1446}}), it is described as “at Kume-gō, western mountain tail of Mt. Unebi.” But maps of the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era place it on the summit, meaning it was moved from the foot to the summit between Bun'an and Tenshō. The shrine name evolved from Unebi-za Yamaguchi Shrine; in the Edo period it was also called “Unebi Myōjin” and “Unebi-yama Jingū-sha.” In {{ill|lt=1940|Q18633|WD=Q18633|1=1940}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 15), when major expansion works were carried out at {{ill|lt=Kashihara Jingū|Q710333|WD=Q710333|1=Kashihara Shrine}} on the eastern foot of Mt. Unebi, it was deemed, in the climate of the high tide of {{ill|lt=imperial-state historiography|Q5368837|WD=Q5368837|1=Kōkoku shikan}}, that looking down over Kashihara Jingū and Emperor Jinmu’s mausoleum would “defile divine authority”According to the explanatory notice at the torii of Unebiyamaguchi Shrine.. The Japanese government therefore ordered relocation to the western foot (current site). At that time it was promoted from {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} to prefectural shrine. === Rituals === A special ritual called “Hani-tsuchi ritual” is held. It takes soil from the summit of Mt. Unebi for making earthenware used at {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} in the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} (February) and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} (November). On that occasion, those performing the rite customarily prepare their ritual garments at {{ill|lt=Kawamata Shrine|Q11553360|WD=Q11553360|1=Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}} before heading to this shrine. === Events === * January 1: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=New Year’s Day festival|1=UNKNOWN}} * February 28: {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} - Otaue festival (onda matsuri) * April 16: Spring grand festival - Daidai festival * July 28: Summer grand festival - Densoso festival[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/saijiki/7_densosomatsuri.html Densoso festival (Kashihara City official site)] * July 29: Great purification festival * December 3: New grain thanksgiving festival * 1st of every month: {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}}, 15th: Birth report festival === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === External links === {{commons-inline|Category:Unebiyamaguchi-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うねひやまくちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unebiyamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unebiyamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Unebiyamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大うねひやまくちしんしや]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=April 16|Q2519|WD=Q2519|1=April 16}} | 神事=Hani-tsuchi ritual | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Unebi-yama
Yamaguchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Unebiyamaguchi Shrine''' (うねびやまぐちじんじゃ, おむねやまじんじゃOn the signboard in front of the Mt. Unebi trailhead by the shrine, “畝火山口” is given the reading “おむねやま”.) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} on the western foot of {{ill|lt=Mount Unebi|Q17606448|WD=Q17606448|1=Mount Unebi}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is popularly called “Omine-yama.” It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was prefectural shrine. It is one of the six Yamaguchi shrines in Yamato listed in the norito of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} ({{ill|lt=Asuka Shrine#Precincts|Q11665959|WD=Q11665959|lt=Asuka|1=Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine}}, Ishimura, Osa, Hase, '''Unebi''', and {{ill|lt=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11610732|WD=Q11610732|lt=Miminashi|1=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine}}), and one of the 85 shrines of the rain-prayer rites in the {{ill|lt=special festivals|Q11612499|WD=Q11612499|1=special festivals}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}), {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}}, and {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi three deities|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}. Based on the tradition that Empress Jingū gave birth to Emperor Ōjin, it is worshiped as a deity of safe childbirth. From examples of other {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi shrines|Q11467399|WD=Q11467399|1=Yamaguchi shrines}} and the shrine’s character, the original deity is thought to have been {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}. Today Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in the auxiliary Ōyamatsumi Shrine. === History === The origin of the foundation is unknown. The first documentary appearance is in ''Shinshō Kakuchokufu-shō'', which records one shrine household granted in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|1=Daidō}} 1). According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', in {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}} ({{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|1=Jōgan}} 1), on {{ill|lt=January 27 (lunar)|Q839032|WD=Q839032|lt=1st month 27th day|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)}}, it received {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of Senior Fifth Lower, and on {{ill|lt=September 8 (lunar)|Q840354|WD=Q840354|lt=September 8|1=September 8 (Lunisolar)}} of the same year an {{ill|lt=imperial envoy with offerings|Q11445508|WD=Q11445508|1=Hōhei}} was dispatched for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=rain prayer|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it appears as “Unebi Yamaguchi-za Shrine, Takachi District, Yamato Province,” is listed as a great shrine, and is recorded as receiving offerings at monthly and Niiname rites. Originally it stood on the western foot of Mt. Unebi. In the ''Gogun Jinjaki'' of {{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1446|Q6430|WD=Q6430|1=1446}}), it is described as “at Kume-gō, western mountain tail of Mt. Unebi.” But maps of the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era place it on the summit, meaning it was moved from the foot to the summit between Bun'an and Tenshō. The shrine name evolved from Unebi-za Yamaguchi Shrine; in the Edo period it was also called “Unebi Myōjin” and “Unebi-yama Jingū-sha.” In {{ill|lt=1940|Q18633|WD=Q18633|1=1940}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 15), when major expansion works were carried out at {{ill|lt=Kashihara Jingū|Q710333|WD=Q710333|1=Kashihara Shrine}} on the eastern foot of Mt. Unebi, it was deemed, in the climate of the high tide of {{ill|lt=imperial-state historiography|Q5368837|WD=Q5368837|1=Kōkoku shikan}}, that looking down over Kashihara Jingū and Emperor Jinmu’s mausoleum would “defile divine authority”According to the explanatory notice at the torii of Unebiyamaguchi Shrine.. The Japanese government therefore ordered relocation to the western foot (current site). At that time it was promoted from {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} to prefectural shrine. === Rituals === A special ritual called “Hani-tsuchi ritual” is held. It takes soil from the summit of Mt. Unebi for making earthenware used at {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} in the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} (February) and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} (November). On that occasion, those performing the rite customarily prepare their ritual garments at {{ill|lt=Kawamata Shrine|Q11553360|WD=Q11553360|1=Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}} before heading to this shrine. === Events === * January 1: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=New Year’s Day festival|1=UNKNOWN}} * February 28: {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} - Otaue festival (onda matsuri) * April 16: Spring grand festival - Daidai festival * July 28: Summer grand festival - Densoso festival[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/saijiki/7_densosomatsuri.html Densoso festival (Kashihara City official site)] * July 29: Great purification festival * December 3: New grain thanksgiving festival * 1st of every month: {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}}, 15th: Birth report festival === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === External links === {{commons-inline|Category:Unebiyamaguchi-jinja}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unebiyamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unebiyamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Unebiyamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大うねひやまくちしんしや]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{commons category|Category:Unebiyamaguchi-jinja}} [[JA:畝火山口神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11578590}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine | 画像=[[file:Unebiyamaguchi-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Ōtani-chō 248-5 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|29|33|N|135|46|52|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}
{{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（great）
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=April 16|Q2519|WD=Q2519|1=April 16}} | 神事=Hani-tsuchi ritual | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Unebi-yama
Yamaguchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Unebiyamaguchi Shrine''' (うねびやまぐちじんじゃ, おむねやまじんじゃOn the signboard in front of the Mt. Unebi trailhead by the shrine, “畝火山口” is given the reading “おむねやま”.) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} on the western foot of {{ill|lt=Mount Unebi|Q17606448|WD=Q17606448|1=Mount Unebi}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is popularly called “Omine-yama.” It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was prefectural shrine. It is one of the six Yamaguchi shrines in Yamato listed in the norito of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} ({{ill|lt=Asuka Shrine#Precincts|Q11665959|WD=Q11665959|lt=Asuka|1=Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine}}, Ishimura, Osa, Hase, '''Unebi''', and {{ill|lt=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11610732|WD=Q11610732|lt=Miminashi|1=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine}}), and one of the 85 shrines of the rain-prayer rites in the {{ill|lt=special festivals|Q11612499|WD=Q11612499|1=special festivals}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}), {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}}, and {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi three deities|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}. Based on the tradition that Empress Jingū gave birth to Emperor Ōjin, it is worshiped as a deity of safe childbirth. From examples of other {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi shrines|Q11467399|WD=Q11467399|1=Yamaguchi shrines}} and the shrine’s character, the original deity is thought to have been {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}. Today Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in the auxiliary Ōyamatsumi Shrine. === History === The origin of the foundation is unknown. The first documentary appearance is in ''Shinshō Kakuchokufu-shō'', which records one shrine household granted in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|1=Daidō}} 1). According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', in {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}} ({{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|1=Jōgan}} 1), on {{ill|lt=January 27 (lunar)|Q839032|WD=Q839032|lt=1st month 27th day|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)}}, it received {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of Senior Fifth Lower, and on {{ill|lt=September 8 (lunar)|Q840354|WD=Q840354|lt=September 8|1=September 8 (Lunisolar)}} of the same year an {{ill|lt=imperial envoy with offerings|Q11445508|WD=Q11445508|1=Hōhei}} was dispatched for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=rain prayer|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it appears as “Unebi Yamaguchi-za Shrine, Takachi District, Yamato Province,” is listed as a great shrine, and is recorded as receiving offerings at monthly and Niiname rites. Originally it stood on the western foot of Mt. Unebi. In the ''Gogun Jinjaki'' of {{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1446|Q6430|WD=Q6430|1=1446}}), it is described as “at Kume-gō, western mountain tail of Mt. Unebi.” But maps of the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era place it on the summit, meaning it was moved from the foot to the summit between Bun'an and Tenshō. The shrine name evolved from Unebi-za Yamaguchi Shrine; in the Edo period it was also called “Unebi Myōjin” and “Unebi-yama Jingū-sha.” In {{ill|lt=1940|Q18633|WD=Q18633|1=1940}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 15), when major expansion works were carried out at {{ill|lt=Kashihara Jingū|Q710333|WD=Q710333|1=Kashihara Shrine}} on the eastern foot of Mt. Unebi, it was deemed, in the climate of the high tide of {{ill|lt=imperial-state historiography|Q5368837|WD=Q5368837|1=Kōkoku shikan}}, that looking down over Kashihara Jingū and Emperor Jinmu’s mausoleum would “defile divine authority”According to the explanatory notice at the torii of Unebiyamaguchi Shrine.. The Japanese government therefore ordered relocation to the western foot (current site). At that time it was promoted from {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} to prefectural shrine. === Rituals === A special ritual called “Hani-tsuchi ritual” is held. It takes soil from the summit of Mt. Unebi for making earthenware used at {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} in the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} (February) and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} (November). On that occasion, those performing the rite customarily prepare their ritual garments at {{ill|lt=Kawamata Shrine|Q11553360|WD=Q11553360|1=Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}} before heading to this shrine. === Events === * January 1: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=New Year’s Day festival|1=UNKNOWN}} * February 28: {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} - Otaue festival (onda matsuri) * April 16: Spring grand festival - Daidai festival * July 28: Summer grand festival - Densoso festival[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/saijiki/7_densosomatsuri.html Densoso festival (Kashihara City official site)] * July 29: Great purification festival * December 3: New grain thanksgiving festival * 1st of every month: {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}}, 15th: Birth report festival === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === External links === {{commons-inline|Category:Unebiyamaguchi-jinja}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unebiyamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unebiyamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Unebiyamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{commons category|Category:Unebiyamaguchi-jinja}} [[JA:畝火山口神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11578590}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine | 画像=[[file:Unebiyamaguchi-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Ōtani-chō 248-5 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|29|33|N|135|46|52|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}
{{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（great）
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=April 16|Q2519|WD=Q2519|1=April 16}} | 神事=Hani-tsuchi ritual | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Unebi-yama
Yamaguchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Unebiyamaguchi Shrine''' (うねびやまぐちじんじゃ, おむねやまじんじゃOn the signboard in front of the Mt. Unebi trailhead by the shrine, “畝火山口” is given the reading “おむねやま”.) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} on the western foot of {{ill|lt=Mount Unebi|Q17606448|WD=Q17606448|1=Mount Unebi}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is popularly called “Omine-yama.” It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was prefectural shrine. It is one of the six Yamaguchi shrines in Yamato listed in the norito of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} ({{ill|lt=Asuka Shrine#Precincts|Q11665959|WD=Q11665959|lt=Asuka|1=Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine}}, Ishimura, Osa, Hase, '''Unebi''', and {{ill|lt=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11610732|WD=Q11610732|lt=Miminashi|1=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine}}), and one of the 85 shrines of the rain-prayer rites in the {{ill|lt=special festivals|Q11612499|WD=Q11612499|1=special festivals}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}), {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}}, and {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi three deities|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}. Based on the tradition that Empress Jingū gave birth to Emperor Ōjin, it is worshiped as a deity of safe childbirth. From examples of other {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi shrines|Q11467399|WD=Q11467399|1=Yamaguchi shrines}} and the shrine’s character, the original deity is thought to have been {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}. Today Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in the auxiliary Ōyamatsumi Shrine. === History === The origin of the foundation is unknown. The first documentary appearance is in ''Shinshō Kakuchokufu-shō'', which records one shrine household granted in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|1=Daidō}} 1). According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', in {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}} ({{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|1=Jōgan}} 1), on {{ill|lt=January 27 (lunar)|Q839032|WD=Q839032|lt=1st month 27th day|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)}}, it received {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of Senior Fifth Lower, and on {{ill|lt=September 8 (lunar)|Q840354|WD=Q840354|lt=September 8|1=September 8 (Lunisolar)}} of the same year an {{ill|lt=imperial envoy with offerings|Q11445508|WD=Q11445508|1=Hōhei}} was dispatched for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=rain prayer|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it appears as “Unebi Yamaguchi-za Shrine, Takachi District, Yamato Province,” is listed as a great shrine, and is recorded as receiving offerings at monthly and Niiname rites. Originally it stood on the western foot of Mt. Unebi. In the ''Gogun Jinjaki'' of {{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1446|Q6430|WD=Q6430|1=1446}}), it is described as “at Kume-gō, western mountain tail of Mt. Unebi.” But maps of the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era place it on the summit, meaning it was moved from the foot to the summit between Bun'an and Tenshō. The shrine name evolved from Unebi-za Yamaguchi Shrine; in the Edo period it was also called “Unebi Myōjin” and “Unebi-yama Jingū-sha.” In {{ill|lt=1940|Q18633|WD=Q18633|1=1940}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 15), when major expansion works were carried out at {{ill|lt=Kashihara Jingū|Q710333|WD=Q710333|1=Kashihara Shrine}} on the eastern foot of Mt. Unebi, it was deemed, in the climate of the high tide of {{ill|lt=imperial-state historiography|Q5368837|WD=Q5368837|1=Kōkoku shikan}}, that looking down over Kashihara Jingū and Emperor Jinmu’s mausoleum would “defile divine authority”According to the explanatory notice at the torii of Unebiyamaguchi Shrine.. The Japanese government therefore ordered relocation to the western foot (current site). At that time it was promoted from {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} to prefectural shrine. === Rituals === A special ritual called “Hani-tsuchi ritual” is held. It takes soil from the summit of Mt. Unebi for making earthenware used at {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} in the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} (February) and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} (November). On that occasion, those performing the rite customarily prepare their ritual garments at {{ill|lt=Kawamata Shrine|Q11553360|WD=Q11553360|1=Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}} before heading to this shrine. === Events === * January 1: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=New Year’s Day festival|1=UNKNOWN}} * February 28: {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} - Otaue festival (onda matsuri) * April 16: Spring grand festival - Daidai festival * July 28: Summer grand festival - Densoso festival[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/saijiki/7_densosomatsuri.html Densoso festival (Kashihara City official site)] * July 29: Great purification festival * December 3: New grain thanksgiving festival * 1st of every month: {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}}, 15th: Birth report festival === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === External links === {{commons-inline|Category:Unebiyamaguchi-jinja}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unebiyamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unebiyamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Unebiyamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{commons category|Category:Unebiyamaguchi-jinja}} [[JA:畝火山口神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11578590}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine | 画像=[[file:Unebiyamaguchi-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Ōtani-chō 248-5 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|29|33|N|135|46|52|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}
{{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（great）
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=April 16|Q2519|WD=Q2519|1=April 16}} | 神事=Hani-tsuchi ritual | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Unebi-yama
Yamaguchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Unebiyamaguchi Shrine''' (うねびやまぐちじんじゃ, おむねやまじんじゃOn the signboard in front of the Mt. Unebi trailhead by the shrine, “畝火山口” is given the reading “おむねやま”.) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} on the western foot of {{ill|lt=Mount Unebi|Q17606448|WD=Q17606448|1=Mount Unebi}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is popularly called “Omine-yama.” It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was prefectural shrine. It is one of the six Yamaguchi shrines in Yamato listed in the norito of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} ({{ill|lt=Asuka Shrine#Precincts|Q11665959|WD=Q11665959|lt=Asuka|1=Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine}}, Ishimura, Osa, Hase, '''Unebi''', and {{ill|lt=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11610732|WD=Q11610732|lt=Miminashi|1=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine}}), and one of the 85 shrines of the rain-prayer rites in the {{ill|lt=special festivals|Q11612499|WD=Q11612499|1=special festivals}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}), {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}}, and {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi three deities|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}. Based on the tradition that Empress Jingū gave birth to Emperor Ōjin, it is worshiped as a deity of safe childbirth. From examples of other {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi shrines|Q11467399|WD=Q11467399|1=Yamaguchi shrines}} and the shrine’s character, the original deity is thought to have been {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}. Today Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in the auxiliary Ōyamatsumi Shrine. === History === The origin of the foundation is unknown. The first documentary appearance is in ''Shinshō Kakuchokufu-shō'', which records one shrine household granted in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|1=Daidō}} 1). According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', in {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}} ({{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|1=Jōgan}} 1), on {{ill|lt=January 27 (lunar)|Q839032|WD=Q839032|lt=1st month 27th day|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)}}, it received {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of Senior Fifth Lower, and on {{ill|lt=September 8 (lunar)|Q840354|WD=Q840354|lt=September 8|1=September 8 (Lunisolar)}} of the same year an {{ill|lt=imperial envoy with offerings|Q11445508|WD=Q11445508|1=Hōhei}} was dispatched for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=rain prayer|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it appears as “Unebi Yamaguchi-za Shrine, Takachi District, Yamato Province,” is listed as a great shrine, and is recorded as receiving offerings at monthly and Niiname rites. Originally it stood on the western foot of Mt. Unebi. In the ''Gogun Jinjaki'' of {{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1446|Q6430|WD=Q6430|1=1446}}), it is described as “at Kume-gō, western mountain tail of Mt. Unebi.” But maps of the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era place it on the summit, meaning it was moved from the foot to the summit between Bun'an and Tenshō. The shrine name evolved from Unebi-za Yamaguchi Shrine; in the Edo period it was also called “Unebi Myōjin” and “Unebi-yama Jingū-sha.” In {{ill|lt=1940|Q18633|WD=Q18633|1=1940}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 15), when major expansion works were carried out at {{ill|lt=Kashihara Jingū|Q710333|WD=Q710333|1=Kashihara Shrine}} on the eastern foot of Mt. Unebi, it was deemed, in the climate of the high tide of {{ill|lt=imperial-state historiography|Q5368837|WD=Q5368837|1=Kōkoku shikan}}, that looking down over Kashihara Jingū and Emperor Jinmu’s mausoleum would “defile divine authority”According to the explanatory notice at the torii of Unebiyamaguchi Shrine.. The Japanese government therefore ordered relocation to the western foot (current site). At that time it was promoted from {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} to prefectural shrine. === Rituals === A special ritual called “Hani-tsuchi ritual” is held. It takes soil from the summit of Mt. Unebi for making earthenware used at {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} in the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} (February) and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} (November). On that occasion, those performing the rite customarily prepare their ritual garments at {{ill|lt=Kawamata Shrine|Q11553360|WD=Q11553360|1=Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}} before heading to this shrine. === Events === * January 1: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=New Year’s Day festival|1=UNKNOWN}} * February 28: {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} - Otaue festival (onda matsuri) * April 16: Spring grand festival - Daidai festival * July 28: Summer grand festival - Densoso festival[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/saijiki/7_densosomatsuri.html Densoso festival (Kashihara City official site)] * July 29: Great purification festival * December 3: New grain thanksgiving festival * 1st of every month: {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}}, 15th: Birth report festival === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === External links === {{commons-inline|Category:Unebiyamaguchi-jinja}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unebiyamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unebiyamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Unebiyamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=April 16|Q2519|WD=Q2519|1=April 16}} | 神事=Hani-tsuchi ritual | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Unebi-yama
Yamaguchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Unebiyamaguchi Shrine''' (うねびやまぐちじんじゃ, おむねやまじんじゃOn the signboard in front of the Mt. Unebi trailhead by the shrine, “畝火山口” is given the reading “おむねやま”.) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} on the western foot of {{ill|lt=Mount Unebi|Q17606448|WD=Q17606448|1=Mount Unebi}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is popularly called “Omine-yama.” It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was prefectural shrine. It is one of the six Yamaguchi shrines in Yamato listed in the norito of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} ({{ill|lt=Asuka Shrine#Precincts|Q11665959|WD=Q11665959|lt=Asuka|1=Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine}}, Ishimura, Osa, Hase, '''Unebi''', and {{ill|lt=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11610732|WD=Q11610732|lt=Miminashi|1=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine}}), and one of the 85 shrines of the rain-prayer rites in the {{ill|lt=special festivals|Q11612499|WD=Q11612499|1=special festivals}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}), {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}}, and {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi three deities|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}. Based on the tradition that Empress Jingū gave birth to Emperor Ōjin, it is worshiped as a deity of safe childbirth. From examples of other {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi shrines|Q11467399|WD=Q11467399|1=Yamaguchi shrines}} and the shrine’s character, the original deity is thought to have been {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}. Today Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in the auxiliary Ōyamatsumi Shrine. === History === The origin of the foundation is unknown. The first documentary appearance is in ''Shinshō Kakuchokufu-shō'', which records one shrine household granted in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|1=Daidō}} 1). According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', in {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}} ({{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|1=Jōgan}} 1), on {{ill|lt=January 27 (lunar)|Q839032|WD=Q839032|lt=1st month 27th day|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)}}, it received {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of Senior Fifth Lower, and on {{ill|lt=September 8 (lunar)|Q840354|WD=Q840354|lt=September 8|1=September 8 (Lunisolar)}} of the same year an {{ill|lt=imperial envoy with offerings|Q11445508|WD=Q11445508|1=Hōhei}} was dispatched for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=rain prayer|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it appears as “Unebi Yamaguchi-za Shrine, Takachi District, Yamato Province,” is listed as a great shrine, and is recorded as receiving offerings at monthly and Niiname rites. Originally it stood on the western foot of Mt. Unebi. In the ''Gogun Jinjaki'' of {{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1446|Q6430|WD=Q6430|1=1446}}), it is described as “at Kume-gō, western mountain tail of Mt. Unebi.” But maps of the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era place it on the summit, meaning it was moved from the foot to the summit between Bun'an and Tenshō. The shrine name evolved from Unebi-za Yamaguchi Shrine; in the Edo period it was also called “Unebi Myōjin” and “Unebi-yama Jingū-sha.” In {{ill|lt=1940|Q18633|WD=Q18633|1=1940}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 15), when major expansion works were carried out at {{ill|lt=Kashihara Jingū|Q710333|WD=Q710333|1=Kashihara Shrine}} on the eastern foot of Mt. Unebi, it was deemed, in the climate of the high tide of {{ill|lt=imperial-state historiography|Q5368837|WD=Q5368837|1=Kōkoku shikan}}, that looking down over Kashihara Jingū and Emperor Jinmu’s mausoleum would “defile divine authority”According to the explanatory notice at the torii of Unebiyamaguchi Shrine.. The Japanese government therefore ordered relocation to the western foot (current site). At that time it was promoted from {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} to prefectural shrine. === Rituals === A special ritual called “Hani-tsuchi ritual” is held. It takes soil from the summit of Mt. Unebi for making earthenware used at {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} in the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} (February) and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} (November). On that occasion, those performing the rite customarily prepare their ritual garments at {{ill|lt=Kawamata Shrine|Q11553360|WD=Q11553360|1=Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}} before heading to this shrine. === Events === * January 1: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=New Year’s Day festival|1=UNKNOWN}} * February 28: {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} - Otaue festival (onda matsuri) * April 16: Spring grand festival - Daidai festival * July 28: Summer grand festival - Densoso festival[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/saijiki/7_densosomatsuri.html Densoso festival (Kashihara City official site)] * July 29: Great purification festival * December 3: New grain thanksgiving festival * 1st of every month: {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}}, 15th: Birth report festival === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === External links === {{commons-inline|Category:Unebiyamaguchi-jinja}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unebiyamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unebiyamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Unebiyamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{commons category|Category:Unebiyamaguchi-jinja}} [[JA:畝火山口神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11578590}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine | 画像=[[file:Unebiyamaguchi-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Ōtani-chō 248-5 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|29|33|N|135|46|52|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}
{{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（great）
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=April 16|Q2519|WD=Q2519|1=April 16}} | 神事=Hani-tsuchi ritual | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Unebi-yama
Yamaguchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Unebiyamaguchi Shrine''' (うねびやまぐちじんじゃ, おむねやまじんじゃOn the signboard in front of the Mt. Unebi trailhead by the shrine, “畝火山口” is given the reading “おむねやま”.) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} on the western foot of {{ill|lt=Mount Unebi|Q17606448|WD=Q17606448|1=Mount Unebi}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is popularly called “Omine-yama.” It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was prefectural shrine. It is one of the six Yamaguchi shrines in Yamato listed in the norito of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} ({{ill|lt=Asuka Shrine#Precincts|Q11665959|WD=Q11665959|lt=Asuka|1=Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine}}, Ishimura, Osa, Hase, '''Unebi''', and {{ill|lt=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11610732|WD=Q11610732|lt=Miminashi|1=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine}}), and one of the 85 shrines of the rain-prayer rites in the {{ill|lt=special festivals|Q11612499|WD=Q11612499|1=special festivals}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}), {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}}, and {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi three deities|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}. Based on the tradition that Empress Jingū gave birth to Emperor Ōjin, it is worshiped as a deity of safe childbirth. From examples of other {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi shrines|Q11467399|WD=Q11467399|1=Yamaguchi shrines}} and the shrine’s character, the original deity is thought to have been {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}. Today Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in the auxiliary Ōyamatsumi Shrine. === History === The origin of the foundation is unknown. The first documentary appearance is in ''Shinshō Kakuchokufu-shō'', which records one shrine household granted in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|1=Daidō}} 1). According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', in {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}} ({{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|1=Jōgan}} 1), on {{ill|lt=January 27 (lunar)|Q839032|WD=Q839032|lt=1st month 27th day|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)}}, it received {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of Senior Fifth Lower, and on {{ill|lt=September 8 (lunar)|Q840354|WD=Q840354|lt=September 8|1=September 8 (Lunisolar)}} of the same year an {{ill|lt=imperial envoy with offerings|Q11445508|WD=Q11445508|1=Hōhei}} was dispatched for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=rain prayer|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it appears as “Unebi Yamaguchi-za Shrine, Takachi District, Yamato Province,” is listed as a great shrine, and is recorded as receiving offerings at monthly and Niiname rites. Originally it stood on the western foot of Mt. Unebi. In the ''Gogun Jinjaki'' of {{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1446|Q6430|WD=Q6430|1=1446}}), it is described as “at Kume-gō, western mountain tail of Mt. Unebi.” But maps of the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era place it on the summit, meaning it was moved from the foot to the summit between Bun'an and Tenshō. The shrine name evolved from Unebi-za Yamaguchi Shrine; in the Edo period it was also called “Unebi Myōjin” and “Unebi-yama Jingū-sha.” In {{ill|lt=1940|Q18633|WD=Q18633|1=1940}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 15), when major expansion works were carried out at {{ill|lt=Kashihara Jingū|Q710333|WD=Q710333|1=Kashihara Shrine}} on the eastern foot of Mt. Unebi, it was deemed, in the climate of the high tide of {{ill|lt=imperial-state historiography|Q5368837|WD=Q5368837|1=Kōkoku shikan}}, that looking down over Kashihara Jingū and Emperor Jinmu’s mausoleum would “defile divine authority”According to the explanatory notice at the torii of Unebiyamaguchi Shrine.. The Japanese government therefore ordered relocation to the western foot (current site). At that time it was promoted from {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} to prefectural shrine. === Rituals === A special ritual called “Hani-tsuchi ritual” is held. It takes soil from the summit of Mt. Unebi for making earthenware used at {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} in the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} (February) and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} (November). On that occasion, those performing the rite customarily prepare their ritual garments at {{ill|lt=Kawamata Shrine|Q11553360|WD=Q11553360|1=Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}} before heading to this shrine. === Events === * January 1: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=New Year’s Day festival|1=UNKNOWN}} * February 28: {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} - Otaue festival (onda matsuri) * April 16: Spring grand festival - Daidai festival * July 28: Summer grand festival - Densoso festival[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/saijiki/7_densosomatsuri.html Densoso festival (Kashihara City official site)] * July 29: Great purification festival * December 3: New grain thanksgiving festival * 1st of every month: {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}}, 15th: Birth report festival === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === External links === {{commons-inline|Category:Unebiyamaguchi-jinja}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unebiyamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unebiyamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Unebiyamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{commons category|Category:Unebiyamaguchi-jinja}} [[JA:畝火山口神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11578590}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine | 画像=[[file:Unebiyamaguchi-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Ōtani-chō 248-5 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|29|33|N|135|46|52|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}
{{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（great）
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=April 16|Q2519|WD=Q2519|1=April 16}} | 神事=Hani-tsuchi ritual | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Unebi-yama
Yamaguchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Unebiyamaguchi Shrine''' (うねびやまぐちじんじゃ, おむねやまじんじゃOn the signboard in front of the Mt. Unebi trailhead by the shrine, “畝火山口” is given the reading “おむねやま”.) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} on the western foot of {{ill|lt=Mount Unebi|Q17606448|WD=Q17606448|1=Mount Unebi}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is popularly called “Omine-yama.” It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was prefectural shrine. It is one of the six Yamaguchi shrines in Yamato listed in the norito of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} ({{ill|lt=Asuka Shrine#Precincts|Q11665959|WD=Q11665959|lt=Asuka|1=Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine}}, Ishimura, Osa, Hase, '''Unebi''', and {{ill|lt=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11610732|WD=Q11610732|lt=Miminashi|1=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine}}), and one of the 85 shrines of the rain-prayer rites in the {{ill|lt=special festivals|Q11612499|WD=Q11612499|1=special festivals}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}), {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}}, and {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi three deities|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}. Based on the tradition that Empress Jingū gave birth to Emperor Ōjin, it is worshiped as a deity of safe childbirth. From examples of other {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi shrines|Q11467399|WD=Q11467399|1=Yamaguchi shrines}} and the shrine’s character, the original deity is thought to have been {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}. Today Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in the auxiliary Ōyamatsumi Shrine. === History === The origin of the foundation is unknown. The first documentary appearance is in ''Shinshō Kakuchokufu-shō'', which records one shrine household granted in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|1=Daidō}} 1). According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', in {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}} ({{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|1=Jōgan}} 1), on {{ill|lt=January 27 (lunar)|Q839032|WD=Q839032|lt=1st month 27th day|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)}}, it received {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of Senior Fifth Lower, and on {{ill|lt=September 8 (lunar)|Q840354|WD=Q840354|lt=September 8|1=September 8 (Lunisolar)}} of the same year an {{ill|lt=imperial envoy with offerings|Q11445508|WD=Q11445508|1=Hōhei}} was dispatched for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=rain prayer|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it appears as “Unebi Yamaguchi-za Shrine, Takachi District, Yamato Province,” is listed as a great shrine, and is recorded as receiving offerings at monthly and Niiname rites. Originally it stood on the western foot of Mt. Unebi. In the ''Gogun Jinjaki'' of {{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1446|Q6430|WD=Q6430|1=1446}}), it is described as “at Kume-gō, western mountain tail of Mt. Unebi.” But maps of the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era place it on the summit, meaning it was moved from the foot to the summit between Bun'an and Tenshō. The shrine name evolved from Unebi-za Yamaguchi Shrine; in the Edo period it was also called “Unebi Myōjin” and “Unebi-yama Jingū-sha.” In {{ill|lt=1940|Q18633|WD=Q18633|1=1940}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 15), when major expansion works were carried out at {{ill|lt=Kashihara Jingū|Q710333|WD=Q710333|1=Kashihara Shrine}} on the eastern foot of Mt. Unebi, it was deemed, in the climate of the high tide of {{ill|lt=imperial-state historiography|Q5368837|WD=Q5368837|1=Kōkoku shikan}}, that looking down over Kashihara Jingū and Emperor Jinmu’s mausoleum would “defile divine authority”According to the explanatory notice at the torii of Unebiyamaguchi Shrine.. The Japanese government therefore ordered relocation to the western foot (current site). At that time it was promoted from {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} to prefectural shrine. === Rituals === A special ritual called “Hani-tsuchi ritual” is held. It takes soil from the summit of Mt. Unebi for making earthenware used at {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} in the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} (February) and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} (November). On that occasion, those performing the rite customarily prepare their ritual garments at {{ill|lt=Kawamata Shrine|Q11553360|WD=Q11553360|1=Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}} before heading to this shrine. === Events === * January 1: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=New Year’s Day festival|1=UNKNOWN}} * February 28: {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} - Otaue festival (onda matsuri) * April 16: Spring grand festival - Daidai festival * July 28: Summer grand festival - Densoso festival[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/saijiki/7_densosomatsuri.html Densoso festival (Kashihara City official site)] * July 29: Great purification festival * December 3: New grain thanksgiving festival * 1st of every month: {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}}, 15th: Birth report festival === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === External links === {{commons-inline|Category:Unebiyamaguchi-jinja}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unebiyamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unebiyamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Unebiyamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]]

{{commons category|Category:Unebiyamaguchi-jinja}} [[JA:畝火山口神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11578590}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine | 画像=[[file:Unebiyamaguchi-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Ōtani-chō 248-5 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|29|33|N|135|46|52|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}
{{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（great）
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=April 16|Q2519|WD=Q2519|1=April 16}} | 神事=Hani-tsuchi ritual | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Unebi-yama
Yamaguchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Unebiyamaguchi Shrine''' (うねびやまぐちじんじゃ, おむねやまじんじゃOn the signboard in front of the Mt. Unebi trailhead by the shrine, “畝火山口” is given the reading “おむねやま”.) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} on the western foot of {{ill|lt=Mount Unebi|Q17606448|WD=Q17606448|1=Mount Unebi}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is popularly called “Omine-yama.” It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was prefectural shrine. It is one of the six Yamaguchi shrines in Yamato listed in the norito of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} ({{ill|lt=Asuka Shrine#Precincts|Q11665959|WD=Q11665959|lt=Asuka|1=Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine}}, Ishimura, Osa, Hase, '''Unebi''', and {{ill|lt=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11610732|WD=Q11610732|lt=Miminashi|1=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine}}), and one of the 85 shrines of the rain-prayer rites in the {{ill|lt=special festivals|Q11612499|WD=Q11612499|1=special festivals}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}), {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}}, and {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi three deities|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}. Based on the tradition that Empress Jingū gave birth to Emperor Ōjin, it is worshiped as a deity of safe childbirth. From examples of other {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi shrines|Q11467399|WD=Q11467399|1=Yamaguchi shrines}} and the shrine’s character, the original deity is thought to have been {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}. Today Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in the auxiliary Ōyamatsumi Shrine. === History === The origin of the foundation is unknown. The first documentary appearance is in ''Shinshō Kakuchokufu-shō'', which records one shrine household granted in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|1=Daidō}} 1). According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', in {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}} ({{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|1=Jōgan}} 1), on {{ill|lt=January 27 (lunar)|Q839032|WD=Q839032|lt=1st month 27th day|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)}}, it received {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of Senior Fifth Lower, and on {{ill|lt=September 8 (lunar)|Q840354|WD=Q840354|lt=September 8|1=September 8 (Lunisolar)}} of the same year an {{ill|lt=imperial envoy with offerings|Q11445508|WD=Q11445508|1=Hōhei}} was dispatched for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=rain prayer|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it appears as “Unebi Yamaguchi-za Shrine, Takachi District, Yamato Province,” is listed as a great shrine, and is recorded as receiving offerings at monthly and Niiname rites. Originally it stood on the western foot of Mt. Unebi. In the ''Gogun Jinjaki'' of {{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1446|Q6430|WD=Q6430|1=1446}}), it is described as “at Kume-gō, western mountain tail of Mt. Unebi.” But maps of the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era place it on the summit, meaning it was moved from the foot to the summit between Bun'an and Tenshō. The shrine name evolved from Unebi-za Yamaguchi Shrine; in the Edo period it was also called “Unebi Myōjin” and “Unebi-yama Jingū-sha.” In {{ill|lt=1940|Q18633|WD=Q18633|1=1940}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 15), when major expansion works were carried out at {{ill|lt=Kashihara Jingū|Q710333|WD=Q710333|1=Kashihara Shrine}} on the eastern foot of Mt. Unebi, it was deemed, in the climate of the high tide of {{ill|lt=imperial-state historiography|Q5368837|WD=Q5368837|1=Kōkoku shikan}}, that looking down over Kashihara Jingū and Emperor Jinmu’s mausoleum would “defile divine authority”According to the explanatory notice at the torii of Unebiyamaguchi Shrine.. The Japanese government therefore ordered relocation to the western foot (current site). At that time it was promoted from {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} to prefectural shrine. === Rituals === A special ritual called “Hani-tsuchi ritual” is held. It takes soil from the summit of Mt. Unebi for making earthenware used at {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} in the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} (February) and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} (November). On that occasion, those performing the rite customarily prepare their ritual garments at {{ill|lt=Kawamata Shrine|Q11553360|WD=Q11553360|1=Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}} before heading to this shrine. === Events === * January 1: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=New Year’s Day festival|1=UNKNOWN}} * February 28: {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} - Otaue festival (onda matsuri) * April 16: Spring grand festival - Daidai festival * July 28: Summer grand festival - Densoso festival[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/saijiki/7_densosomatsuri.html Densoso festival (Kashihara City official site)] * July 29: Great purification festival * December 3: New grain thanksgiving festival * 1st of every month: {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}}, 15th: Birth report festival === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === External links === {{commons-inline|Category:Unebiyamaguchi-jinja}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unebiyamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unebiyamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unebiyamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]]
